title: Park transform sign convention error
description: The Iq row of the Park transform uses a negative sin term: Iq = -Iα·sin(θ) + Iβ·cos(θ). Reversing the sign produces a torque in the wrong direction that is difficult to detect from current magnitudes alone.
trigger: Any edit to TransformsClarkePark, Park transform equations, or a new FOC implementation that reimplements the transform rather than reusing TransformsClarkePark.
mitigation: Always reuse TransformsClarkePark from core/foc/implementations/. If implementing manually, verify against the identity: applying Park then inverse Park to a known vector must recover the original. Add a round-trip unit test.

title: Mechanical vs electrical angle confusion
description: Motor electrical angle θe = θm × pole_pairs. Using the raw encoder (mechanical) angle directly in Park/inverse-Park produces incorrect field orientation. The error is proportional to (pole_pairs - 1) and grows with pole count.
trigger: Any code that reads encoder position and passes it to Clarke/Park transforms, speed estimation, or electrical angle calculations. Adding or changing polePairs configuration.
mitigation: Always multiply mechanical angle by polePairs before passing to transforms. Unit-type alias Radians does not distinguish electrical from mechanical — add a comment at the conversion site. Verify with a test using a known pole-pair count.

title: SVM sector-boundary discontinuity
description: Space Vector Modulation sector detection uses integer division and modulo on a normalized angle. Floating-point precision at the exact sector boundary (0, π/3, 2π/3, π, 4π/3, 5π/3) can produce sector index 0 or 6 instead of the expected 1–6, causing a zero-vector or wrong duty cycle for one control cycle.
trigger: Any modification to SpaceVectorModulation sector detection logic, changes to the angle normalization step, or reimplementing SVM rather than reusing SpaceVectorModulation.
mitigation: Test all 6 sector boundary angles plus a small epsilon offset in both directions. Reuse SpaceVectorModulation — do not reimplement. Clamp sector index to [1,6] after computation as a safety guard.

title: PID integrator without anti-windup
description: A PID controller without integrator clamping or back-calculation accumulates indefinitely during saturation. When the output unsaturates, the stored integral causes a large transient overshoot.
trigger: Any new PidController instantiation, any new current loop, speed loop, or position loop implementation. Any change to existing PID tuning parameters that changes the output limit.
mitigation: Always configure the integral clamp limits when instantiating PidController. Verify with a test: apply a step that saturates the output, hold for several iterations, then remove the step and confirm the integrator has not wound up past the output limit. Cross-reference invariant i016.

title: Encoder angle wraparound in speed estimation
description: Speed is estimated from the angular delta between two successive encoder readings. At the ±π wraparound, a naive delta (θ_new - θ_old) produces a spike of ≈2π instead of a small value near zero, causing a momentary speed spike that saturates the speed controller.
trigger: Any code that computes angular velocity from raw encoder position differences without wraparound handling. New speed estimator implementations. Changes to encoder reading code.
mitigation: Normalize the angular delta to [-π, π] using a wraparound-safe subtraction (e.g., std::remainder or equivalent) before dividing by the time step. Add a unit test that steps the angle through the ±π boundary and verifies smooth speed output.

title: Raw std::sin/std::cos in hot path
description: std::sin and std::cos from <cmath> are software floating-point implementations that typically cost 20–100 cycles each on Cortex-M without FPU sin/cos instructions. Using them in Calculate() can alone consume the entire 400-cycle budget.
trigger: Any #include <cmath> added to a file under core/foc/implementations/. Any sin( or cos( call in a hot-path method. Any new FOC implementation that does not reuse TrigonometricImpl.
mitigation: Use TrigonometricImpl or a lookup table for all trigonometric calls in the hot path. Reuse TransformsClarkePark which already uses the approved fast path. Cross-reference invariant i006.

title: Pure virtual destructor added by reflex
description: When creating a new abstract interface, the instinct is to write virtual ~MyInterface() = 0. This pulls in __cxa_pure_virtual, adds vtable overhead, and increases flash/RAM consumption on MCUs.
trigger: Creating any new class in core/foc/interfaces/, core/platform_abstraction/, or any abstract base class. The pattern 'virtual ~' in a header file.
mitigation: The default is no destructor declaration at all. If polymorphic deletion through the base pointer is needed, use a non-pure virtual destructor with a default body: virtual ~MyInterface() = default. Cross-reference invariant i005.

title: Non-template method body in header
description: Placing a non-template method implementation in a .hpp file causes the function body to be compiled into every translation unit that includes the header, resulting in ODR violations at link time or bloated object files.
trigger: Adding a method body (braces + implementation) in a .hpp file for a class that is not a template. Any new class definition with inline method bodies for non-trivial operations.
mitigation: Move the implementation to the corresponding .cpp file. Only template method bodies are allowed in headers. Cross-reference invariant i012.

title: Missing OPTIMIZE_FOR_SPEED on new hot-path method
description: A new Calculate() or Compute() method without the OPTIMIZE_FOR_SPEED attribute will use the translation-unit default optimization level, not O3/fast-math, even if the file-level pragma is present. The attribute is required in addition to the pragma.
trigger: Adding a new Calculate(), Compute(), or other hot-path method to any file under core/foc/implementations/. Refactoring an existing hot-path method by splitting it.
mitigation: Prefix every hot-path method definition with OPTIMIZE_FOR_SPEED. Include numerical/math/CompilerOptimizations.hpp. Cross-reference invariants i009 and i010.

title: Missing file-level pragma on new hot-path file
description: A new .cpp file under core/foc/implementations/ without the file-level #pragma GCC optimize("O3","fast-math") block will be compiled at the project default level (-Og in debug builds) regardless of per-method attributes.
trigger: Creating any new .cpp or .hpp file under core/foc/implementations/ that contains hot-path code. Copying an existing file and forgetting to carry the pragma.
mitigation: Add the guarded pragma block at the top of every new hot-path file, before any #includes. Cross-reference invariant i009.

title: NiceMock or NaggyMock introduced in tests
description: NiceMock silently swallows unexpected mock calls, hiding test failures. NaggyMock emits warnings but does not fail the test. Both reduce test reliability and can mask regressions that StrictMock would catch immediately.
trigger: Any new mock instantiation in a test file. Copying code from external examples or other projects that use NiceMock. Adding a new test fixture with mock dependencies.
mitigation: Use testing::StrictMock<MockType> exclusively. If a test requires that some calls are optional, use EXPECT_CALL with AnyNumber() on the specific optional call rather than switching to NiceMock. Cross-reference invariant i011.

title: Dynamic STL container include in embedded code
description: Adding #include <vector>, #include <string>, #include <map>, etc. in a file under the embedded runtime paths makes the dynamic container available even if not used directly — and it is easy for a subsequent edit to start using it.
trigger: Any new #include of a dynamic STL header in a file under core/foc/, core/platform_abstraction/, core/state_machine/, or targets/. Code generation or copy-paste from host-side code.
mitigation: Remove the include and replace with the appropriate infra::Bounded* type. Cross-reference invariant i003. Consider adding a CI grep gate on these include patterns in the scoped paths.

title: EXPECT_EQ on floating-point result in tests
description: EXPECT_EQ uses exact equality. Floating-point FOC calculations (transforms, PID outputs, SVM duties) are subject to rounding; exact equality checks will produce flaky or always-failing tests across compilers or optimization levels.
trigger: Any new test assertion on a float, double, Ampere, Radians, Volts, Rpm, or PhasePwmDutyCycles value using EXPECT_EQ.
mitigation: Use EXPECT_NEAR(actual, expected, tolerance) for all floating-point assertions. Choose the tolerance based on the expected numerical precision of the algorithm (typically 1e-4 to 1e-6 for FOC transforms).

title: Behavior change without documentation update
description: Changing a service's observable behavior (state transitions, control mode logic, interface contract, parameter handling) without updating the corresponding documentation/design/<service>.md leaves the doc and code out of sync. Future agents and developers will implement against the stale doc and produce regressions.
trigger: Any commit that modifies core/foc/implementations/, core/services/*, or core/state_machine/ without a corresponding change to a file under documentation/architecture/ or documentation/design/.
mitigation: Before or alongside the code change, update the relevant design doc. If no doc exists, create one from documentation/templates/design.md. Cross-reference invariant i017.

title: Modifying blast-radius header without updating all consumers
description: Headers like FocBase.hpp, PlatformFactory.hpp, and FocStateMachineImpl.hpp are included across the entire codebase. A signature change, added parameter, or changed return type requires updating every consumer in the same change set. Partial updates break the build across multiple targets.
trigger: Any edit to a file listed in .github/memory/sources.jsonl. Any change to a method signature, constructor, or type in core/foc/interfaces/, core/platform_abstraction/PlatformFactory.hpp, or core/state_machine/FocStateMachineImpl.hpp.
mitigation: Before modifying a blast-radius header, read .github/memory/sources.jsonl and identify all importedBy consumers. Include all of them in scope for the change. Build all targets (host, EK-TM4C1294XL, STM32F407G-DISC1) to verify no consumer is broken.
